reason: The core claims are well-supported. The wiki confirms Mundungus appears only in DH Part 1 (absent from OotP and HBP films), his OotP role (abandoning post, triggering the dementor attack) is entirely absent from the films, and the Snape Confundus scene is book-only - the film presents the decoy plan without showing its origin. The personality observations (toad-selling anecdote, pipe-smoking, Fred/George camaraderie) are all book-accurate details absent from the film. The one minor quibble: the scorer says the film has 'two scenes' - the wiki confirms his film role is limited to the locket/Kreacher scene and the Seven Potters scene, which checks out. The narrative_role score of 13/25 seems reasonable given the Confundus scene omission is a real structural loss. Overall the justifications are factually accurate and the 70/100 total is a defensible middle-ground for a character who retains his core opportunistic identity in the film but loses almost all his book texture and his most structurally significant scene.
